headline: SpaceX Is Worth $2 Trillion? What That Might Mean.
summary: SpaceX is worth how much?  Bloomberg reported Thursday that SpaceX was seeking an initial public offering valuation of about $2 trillion, up from earlier estimates of $1.8 trillion.  SpaceX was valued at $1.25 trillion after its merger in February with xAI.

headline: Electric Vehicles Today - Rising Demand Fuels Growth In Magnet Market
summary: The electric vehicle (EV) magnet market is projected to surge from USD 5.3 billion in 2025 to USD 9.5 billion by 2030, driven by the global shift to electric vehicles, government incentives, and stringent emissions regulations. As demand for high-performance permanent magnets, particularly neodymium-iron-boron in traction motors, increases, advancements in motor efficiency and investments in EV manufacturing and infrastructure in Asia Pacific, Europe, and North America fuel this growth. The...

headline: US Stock Market Today: S&P 500 Futures Slip As Strong Jobs Data Meets Rate Pressure
summary: The Morning Bull - US Market Morning Update Friday, Apr, 3 2026 US stock futures are slightly softer this morning, with E-mini S&P 500 contracts modestly in the red, as investors weigh strong US job signals against rising borrowing costs and pricier food and energy. Weekly initial jobless claims sit at 202,000, near a 2 year low, which indicates companies are still holding on to workers and supports expectations for a March payroll gain of about 60,000 jobs and wage growth of 0.3% month on...

Big Tech H-1B Applications Drop Federal data shows Amazon.com Inc.), Alphabet Inc.'s Google, Meta Platforms, Inc., and Microsoft Corp filed significantly fewer H-1B petitions in the first quarter of fiscal 2026, as reported by Business Insider on Wednesday. headline: Alphabet vs. Tesla: This stock is just an 'immense cash flow machine'
summary: Yahoo Finance's "Market Madness" tournament has whittled down the competition of top-performing companies, pairing Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL) against Tesla (TSLA) and Palantir Technologies (PLTR) against Walmart (WMT) in today's matchups.Carnegie Investment Counsel director of research Greg Halter decides who will move on to the Final Four in this clash of the corporations, detailing his reasoning and analysis behind each company's stock and growth drivers.

content.title: Alphabet vs. Tesla: This stock is just an 'immense cash flow machine'
content.description:
Yahoo Finance's "Market Madness" tournament has whittled down the competition of top-performing companies, pairing Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL) against Tesla (TSLA) and Palantir Technologies (PLTR) against Walmart (WMT) in today's matchups.

Carnegie Investment Counsel director of research Greg Halter decides who will move on to the Final Four in this clash of the corporations, detailing his reasoning and analysis behind each company's stock and growth drivers.
content.summary: Yahoo Finance's "Market Madness" tournament has whittled down the competition of top-performing companies, pairing Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL) against Tesla (TSLA) and Palantir Technologies (PLTR) against Walmart (WMT) in today's matchups.Carnegie Investment Counsel director of research Greg Halter decides who will move on to the Final Four in this clash of the corporations, detailing his reasoning and analysis behind each company's stock and growth drivers.

headline: Tesla’s Q1 Delivery Miss And Storage Slowdown Test Cash Generation Hopes
summary: Tesla, Inc. (NasdaqGS:TSLA) reported Q1 2026 vehicle deliveries that missed its own targets, contributing to an inventory buildup of over 50,000 vehicles produced but not yet sold. The company also reported a 38% sequential decline in energy storage deployments in the quarter, after this segment had been framed as a key part of its diversification beyond autos. These developments arrive as Tesla highlights AI, robotics, and physical autonomy as future areas of focus, which raises questions...
